title: [FIRRTL] Enum expressions of type UInt<0> fail to parse
body:
The following FIRRTL snippet adapted from the FIRRTL spec fails to parse:

```
FIRRTL version 4.0.0
circuit Foo:
  module Foo:
    node x = {|a, b, c|}(a)
```

This crashes with: 

```
Assertion failed: (currentSMLoc != SMLoc() && "No .fir file location specified"), function notifyOperationInserted, file FIRParser.cpp, line 1507.
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/circt and include the crash backtrace.
Stack dump:
0.	Program arguments: firtool -parse-only build/spec.md-code-example-110.fir
Stack dump without symbol names (ensure you have llvm-symbolizer in your PATH or set the environment var `LLVM_SYMBOLIZER_PATH` to point to it):
0  firtool                  0x0000000105089860 llvm::sys::PrintStackTrace(llvm::raw_ostream&, int) + 56
1  firtool                  0x0000000105087de4 llvm::sys::RunSignalHandlers() + 112
2  firtool                  0x0000000105089ecc SignalHandler(int) + 304
3  libsystem_platform.dylib 0x0000000199692a24 _sigtramp + 56
4  libsystem_pthread.dylib  0x0000000199663c28 pthread_kill + 288
5  libsystem_c.dylib        0x0000000199571ae8 abort + 180
6  libsystem_c.dylib        0x0000000199570e44 err + 0
7  firtool                  0x0000000105a42bb8 (anonymous namespace)::LazyLocationListener::notifyOperationInserted(mlir::Operation*, mlir::OpBuilder::InsertPoint) (.cold.2) + 0
8  firtool                  0x00000001050eceb0 (anonymous namespace)::LazyLocationListener::notifyOperationInserted(mlir::Operation*, mlir::OpBuilder::InsertPoint) + 104
9  firtool                  0x0000000105360880 mlir::OpBuilder::create(mlir::OperationState const&) + 144
10 firtool                  0x000000010510424c circt::firrtl::ConstantOp mlir::OpBuilder::create<circt::firrtl::ConstantOp, circt::firrtl::IntType&, mlir::IntegerAttr&>(mlir::Location, circt::firrtl::IntType&, mlir::IntegerAttr&) + 148
11 firtool                  0x00000001050f993c (anonymous namespace)::FIRStmtParser::parseEnumExp(mlir::Value&) + 376
12 firtool                  0x00000001050f73c4 (anonymous namespace)::FIRStmtParser::parseExpImpl(mlir::Value&, llvm::Twine const&, bool) + 1724
13 firtool                  0x00000001050ed624 (anonymous namespace)::FIRStmtParser::parseSimpleStmtImpl(unsigned int) + 1784
14 firtool                  0x00000001050ec8ac (anonymous namespace)::FIRStmtParser::parseSimpleStmtBlock(unsigned int) + 120
15 firtool                  0x00000001050ec59c (anonymous namespace)::FIRCircuitParser::parseCircuit(llvm::SmallVectorImpl<llvm::MemoryBuffer const*>&, llvm::SmallVectorImpl<llvm::MemoryBuffer const*>&, mlir::TimingScope&)::$_15::operator()(unsigned long) const + 900
16 firtool                  0x00000001050e1d80 circt::firrtl::importFIRFile(llvm::SourceMgr&, mlir::MLIRContext*, mlir::TimingScope&, circt::firrtl::FIRParserOptions) + 12556
17 firtool                  0x0000000104fc24a8 processBuffer(mlir::MLIRContext&, circt::firtool::FirtoolOptions&, mlir::TimingScope&, llvm::SourceMgr&, std::__1::optional<std::__1::unique_ptr<llvm::ToolOutputFile, std::__1::default_delete<llvm::ToolOutputFile>>>&) + 1212
18 firtool                  0x0000000104fc1f1c processInputSplit(mlir::MLIRContext&, circt::firtool::FirtoolOptions&, mlir::TimingScope&, std::__1::unique_ptr<llvm::MemoryBuffer, std::__1::default_delete<llvm::MemoryBuffer>>, std::__1::optional<std::__1::unique_ptr<llvm::ToolOutputFile, std::__1::default_delete<llvm::ToolOutputFile>>>&) + 352
19 firtool                  0x0000000104fbe03c executeFirtool(mlir::MLIRContext&, circt::firtool::FirtoolOptions&) + 1092
20 firtool                  0x0000000104fbbe58 main + 316
21 dyld                     0x000000019930bf28 start + 2236
zsh: abort      firtool -parse-only build/spec.md-code-example-110.fir
```

The crash is really not that interesting as this is crashing while in an error path where it tries to use a location that is null. 

~The problem is that `parseEnumType` can return a zero-width as the type and this isn't handled in `parseEnumExp`, this hits the error path, then the error path crashes.~ The problem is _actually_ that this tries to create a zero-width constant which crashes and the `SMLoc` is null which causes the error path to crash.
